theory.intro: Because $1\text{ kg} = 1000\text{ g}$, a mass given in kilograms and grams can be written as a single decimal number of kilograms.
facts: $1\text{ kg} = 1000\text{ g}$, so $1\text{ g} = \tfrac{1}{1000}\text{ kg} = 0.001\text{ kg}$.
$3\text{ kg } 250\text{ g} = 3.25\text{ kg}$ — the grams become the decimal part.
steps: Keep the whole kilograms before the decimal point.
Divide the grams by $1000$ to get the decimal part.
